LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. HE ALSO DISCLOSED ABOUT ELIZABETH SALVESON WHOSE NAME APPEARS IN THE PLEADING IS A FORMER PARTNER AND A FAMILY FRIEND. OBJECTION WAS RAISED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AS TO THE LATTER DISCLOSURE. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Police Department, And, Heather Fong Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-23-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
QUINTON B. CUTLIP, ESQ., LAW OFFICES OF MEIS & ASSOCIATES, TEL.415-981-4612 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF
LARRY HECIMOVICH, ESQ., OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. 415-554-3800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS

This cause, having been continued from July 20, 2009, comes on calendar this day for a hearing on the properly noticed motion. The Court, court personnel and counsel are present.

The motion is argued and submitted.

Defendants' demurrer to the 2nd amended complaint as to the first, second and third causes of action is overruled. Defendant has 10 days to file an answer. Plaintiff is to prepare a proposed form of order by July 31, 2009.

Defendants' Requests to take Judicial Notice of the following:
Exhibits A, B, C, G, H & J - No objections;
Exhibits D, E and I - Objections are sustained;
Exhibit F - Court takes judicial notice of the complaint, but not its contents.

Judge Mary E. Wiss, Clerk Clark Banayad, Reporter Diana Chen, CSR #11312

Appearances: Larry Hecimovich, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3933, appearing for Defendant and moving party; Quinton B.
